trace.summary: Addressed static architecture audit findings: tightened discussion/order authorization, hardened moderation and appeal validation, stripped sensitive response fields, and improved observability.
changes: Removed FINANCE_CLERK from elevated order-thread access roles so only REVIEWER and ADMINISTRATOR have blanket access to all order discussions; other roles require ownership.
Added self-flag prevention in comment flagging: users cannot flag their own comments, with a 403 CANNOT_FLAG_OWN_COMMENT error response.
Tightened hidden-content appeal source validation: HIDDEN_CONTENT_BANNER appeals now require the referenced comment to be actually hidden (is_hidden=true), not merely flagged once.
Stripped storageRelativePath from all appeal file API responses (getAppealDetail and uploadAppealFiles) to prevent internal filesystem path leakage to clients.
Removed storageRelativePath from the frontend AppealFile type definition.
Added REVIEWER to the privileged roles in getOrderDetailById for moderation oversight access to order details.
Added structured logging at key business boundaries: discussion comment creation, leader application review decisions, finance withdrawal approvals, and withdrawal blacklist changes.
Documented all .env.example variables with explanatory comments for runtime knobs (LOG_UNHANDLED_ERROR_INCLUDE_STACK, BEHAVIOR_RETENTION_RUN_INTERVAL_MINUTES, DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY).
Updated discussionService test to assert FINANCE_CLERK is now blocked from order threads without ownership, matching the new least-privilege policy.
